title: The Price of Bonney’s Freedom
description: On pages 6–8, this part focuses on Unknown person #4, Bonney, Kuma, Unknown person #5, Unknown person #6 as Kuma’s life, Bonney’s childhood, and the cost of Saturn and the World Government’s cruelty reach their emotional center.
scene: Kuma’s life, Bonney’s childhood, and the cost of Saturn and the World Government’s cruelty reach their emotional center.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Some pirates fled after rapidly a nearby merchant ship!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Your orders are to blow those lowlifies out of the water!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We need to get the wounded to the nearest hospital.

dialogue.speaker: Bonney
dialogue.text: Kuma, come in! What are you doing?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll go on ahead with them. The rest of you should follow as soon as you can.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Thank you so much!! We thought they weren't going to make it!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Wait!! We didn't mean nothin' by it! We'll give everything back!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Everybody, fork over all your loot!! It ain't worth our lives!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The world is full of beauty that can't be captured in photos or picture books.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I long to see your bright, smiling face...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: ...as you experience such wonders for yourself!

dialogue.speaker: Kuma
dialogue.text: Meanwhile, in the kingdom of sorbet...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: I'm sure he's very busy with work.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: that, or the news coos keep messing up.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But Daddy said he'd write a whole bunch of letters...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: NOW, NOW, DEAR. LETTERS HAVE TO GO ON A LONG TREEK OF THEIR OWN, Y'know!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ONE WILL TURN UP SOONER OR ATER, BONNEY, YOU'LL SEE.

dialogue.speaker: Kuma
dialogue.text: Put this in the church's mailbox.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Yep, how many does this make?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He's sending around 10 a month... so that's a fair few...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How's he even finding so much to write about?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Ugh... how mushy can you get?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You're probably sick of me saying this, but...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: I'll be sure to write again soon.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Again? C'mon, Bonney, how about some Jenga instead?

summary: This part advances the Kuma and Bonney Flashback through 55 cleaned dialogue beats, centering the emotional stakes, battle movement, and the wider consequences of Egghead.
